articleTitle: Efficacy of transcranial magnetic stimulation in the treatment of combat-related PTSD: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
takeaways: The study analyzed transcranial magnetic stimulation (TMS) as a treatment specifically for combat veterans with PTSD, a population where 30-50% don't respond adequately to standard medications and therapy

TMS works by targeting fronto-limbic brain circuits that are disrupted in PTSD, offering a non-drug approach that directly stimulates brain regions involved in emotional regulation and memory processing

This systematic review and meta-analysis examined multiple TMS protocols to determine which specific treatment parameters show the strongest evidence for clinical benefit in combat-exposed populations
The research addresses a critical gap in veteran healthcare by evaluating whether TMS can provide meaningful symptom improvement beyond placebo effects in this treatment-resistant population
